market_summary: US equity futures are pointing to a lower open as a surge in oil prices above $100 per barrel reignites inflation fears and pushes Treasury yields to multi-year highs. The dominant tension is between a resilient earnings season and a macro backdrop that is rapidly deteriorating due to geopolitical risk and a hawkish repricing of Fed policy. Investors are positioning for a defensive rotation, with energy and select value sectors likely to outperform while growth and duration-sensitive assets face headwinds.
macro_summary: Stagflationary Shock The market is caught between strong micro fundamentals and a macro environment that is turning hostile due to geopolitical risk and a hawkish Fed. The path of least resistance is lower until oil and yields stabilize. The market is repricing a stagflationary scenario where oil above $100 and rising yields are overwhelming positive earnings news. The dominant tension is between a resilient corporate sector and a rapidly deteriorating macro backdrop, leading to a defensive rotation. The macro outlook has darkened considerably overnight. The combination of oil above $100, a hawkish Fed repricing, and escalating geopolitical tensions points to a stagflationary environment. Growth expectations are being revised down while inflation expectations are moving up, creating a challenging backdrop for risk assets. The dollar is strengthening on a flight to quality, adding further pressure on emerging markets and commodities.
risk_summary: Risk appetite is clearly negative this morning. The VIX is likely to open higher, and we are seeing a classic flight to safety with bids on the dollar, gold, and Treasuries (though yields are rising on inflation fears). Breadth is expected to be poor, with more stocks declining than advancing. The key risk is that the sell-off accelerates if oil continues to spike or if the 10-year yield breaks above key resistance levels. Positioning is likely crowded on the long side after the recent rally, which could exacerbate any downside move.

top_events.title: Oil surges above $100 on Iran conflict escalation
top_events.summary: Oil prices crossed the $100 per barrel threshold as the US-Iran conflict escalated and peace talks failed. This is a stagflationary shock that is repricing risk across all asset classes.

top_events.title: 10-year Treasury yield hits highest since January 2025
top_events.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield rose to its highest level since January 2025, driven by the oil-induced inflation scare and a hawkish repricing of Fed policy.

top_events.title: Odds of Fed rate hike surge on oil spike
top_events.summary: The probability of a Fed rate hike in September has surged as oil prices spike, reigniting inflation fears. The market is now pricing in a more aggressive Fed response.

top_events.title: Treasury yields retreat, 10-year hovers around January 2025 highs
top_events.summary: U.S. Treasury yields retreated on Friday after briefly hitting the highest level since January 2025 the day before.

top_events.title: Trump considers 'massive attack' on Iran
top_events.summary: President Trump told Axios he is considering a 'massive attack' on Iran, escalating the conflict further. This has contributed to the surge in oil prices and risk aversion.

Stocks waver while crude oil prices fall for the first time in a week

top_events.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
top_events.summary: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.

sector_summary: Positive sectors/themes: Energy (XLE), Defense (ITA), Gold Miners (GDX), US Dollar (DXY); Negative sectors/themes: Technology (XLK), Consumer Discretionary (XLY), Real Estate (XLRE), Airlines (JETS); Themes to watch: Stagflation Trade, Defensive Rotation, Value vs. Growth, Commodity Supercycle
